commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Manual maintainer review is needed because the PR changes the runtime plugin support contract and overlaps https://github.com/openclaw/nix-openclaw/pull/100; there is no narrow automated repair finding.; Cleared: The diff is supply-chain-sensitive, but it uses fixed hashes, integrity/SHA checks, tar-member validation, and no runtime package-manager install; no concrete security defect was found. (sha=6e7ae80c86c02aba9ad278f34817f0998f10f042)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds shrinkwrap-backed and ClawHub-resolved materialization for generated OpenClaw runtime plugin locks, regenerates supported plugin packages, and rewrites runtime plugin docs.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR rather than a bug report. Source inspection and the PR body cover the changed lock-generation/build paths instead of a current-main failure reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual maintainer review is needed because the PR changes the runtime plugin support contract and overlaps https://github.com/openclaw/nix-openclaw/pull/100; there is no narrow automated repair finding.

target.title: feat: materialize shrinkwrapped runtime plugins
target.body:
for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels

## Maintainer Decisions

This PR keeps the current shipped user API simple: users enable generated OpenClaw runtime plugin ids with `programs.openclaw.runtimePlugins = [ "discord" "copilot" ];`. Those ids come from the pinned upstream OpenClaw plugin inventory, not from a hand-written nix-openclaw list.

The packageability rule is: nix-openclaw can expose an upstream plugin when the selected artifact can be built reproducibly as a fixed Nix input. Today that means no runtime dependencies, bundled dependencies, or a package-local `npm-shrinkwrap.json` that lets Nix materialize dependencies with an `npmDepsHash`.

There is no fundamental Nix reason arbitrary npm or ClawHub plugins must remain unsupported. The current PR does not yet add the public locked-source option for that. A future or follow-up option should accept a locked source record with plugin id, exact npm/ClawHub source, artifact hash, and `npmDepsHash`, then feed it through the same builder. It must not run `openclaw plugins install` during Home Manager activation.

## Changes

- Adds shrinkwrap-backed npm dependency materialization for OpenClaw runtime plugin roots.
- Adds ClawHub artifact resolution at lock-generation time, then builds the resolved artifact through the same immutable Nix path.
- Expands the generated supported runtime plugin set for the pinned OpenClaw source.
- Rewrites the README runtime plugin section around upstream OpenClaw docs: plugin inventory, install sources, channel config, runtime/provider config, and the Nix packageability rule.
- Explains that `nix/generated/openclaw-runtime-plugins/` is generated from the pinned upstream OpenClaw release inventory files under `scripts/lib/official-external-*`, then mapped to Nix store plugin roots.
- Keeps maintainer diagnostics in the generated report instead of turning skip reasons into product categories.

## User-Facing Support Contract

Upstream install commands map to supported ids:

| Upstream OpenClaw | nix-openclaw |
| --- | --- |
| `openclaw plugins install @openclaw/discord` | `runtimePlugins = [ "discord" ];` |
| `openclaw plugins install clawhub:@openclaw/whatsapp` | `runtimePlugins = [ "whatsapp" ];` |
| `openclaw plugins install @openclaw/copilot` | `runtimePlugins = [ "copilot" ];` |

The current public API exposes generated ids only. Direct arbitrary source specs are a missing locked-source API, not a builder impossibility.

## Current Packageability Gaps

The current OpenClaw 2026.6.1 generated report leaves Weixin, Yuanbao, and WeCom out because their published artifacts declare runtime npm dependencies without `npm-shrinkwrap.json` and without bundled `node_modules`. A plugin-side shrinkwrap or bundled dependency publication should make those packageable by this pipeline.

The remaining PixVerse skipped row is a duplicate upstream row, not a missing packageability class.

## Proof

Latest head: `6e7ae80c8`.

Final-head local proof:

- `git diff --check`
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#checks.aarch64-darwin.runtime-plugin-locks`
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#checks.aarch64-darwin.ci`
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#checks.x86_64-linux.ci`

Earlier implementation proof on this branch also built representative generated plugin packages for Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, Matrix, Codex, ACPX, Memory LanceDB, and GitHub Copilot.

## Merge Notes

This branch overlaps PR #100 at the broader shrinkwrap packaging layer. PR #100 says plugins are out of scope; this PR owns the runtime plugin pipeline. The remaining overlap should be resolved by normal final-head review after merge order is chosen.
target.author: joshp123

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
